methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $400M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Marvel Fusion is a Series B nuclear energy company that has raised $400M+. The valuation analysis draws from a broader nuclear sector comp set of 11 entities—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Type One Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Oklo—though a sufficiently dense same-stage comparable set proved unavailable for direct peer-median triangulation.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable approach here. Using total capital raised ($400M) as a valuation proxy, applying a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $760M. This method accounts for the company's maturity within its funding cycle while remaining conservative relative to downstream-stage multiples observed in the nuclear sector.

The triangulated valuation range is $646M to $874M, with a median point of $760M supported by the single applicable method. The narrow range reflects high confidence in the stage-appropriate multiple, though the analysis is constrained by limited same-stage private comps in nuclear fusion and fission—a structural limitation of the sector's early-stage capital markets. Key uncertainties include technology maturity relative to peers, unit economics trajectory, and regulatory pathway clarity, all of which could justify valuations at either end of the range pending deeper operational diligence.
